headline: Blood Supply Reaches Critical Shortage 14 Times in 2025
context: National Blood Centre declared critical shortage alerts 14 times in 2025 as donation rates lag behind rising demand.

cards.big: National blood supply hit critical shortage levels 14 times in 2025 up from 8 in 2023
cards.sub: Only 2.8% of eligible Malaysians donate blood regularly versus 5% WHO target

cards.big: Surgical procedures were postponed 2,300 times in 2025 due to blood supply shortages
cards.sub: Elective and emergency surgeries both affected during critical shortage periods

cards.big: Walk-in donation model depends on sporadic campaigns rather than scheduled regular donors
cards.sub: Lack of structured retention programs means 68% of first-time donors never return

cards.big: Japan maintains 99% blood self-sufficiency through employer-based regular donation programs
cards.sub: Workplace donation schedules create habitual donors rather than crisis-driven appeals

cards.big: Relying on emergency appeals for blood supply is not a system—it is a recurring crisis
Should employers be required to provide paid time off for blood donation quarterly
